name: slide-mcp-server
description: Ask Claude about your Slide backups: find files, restore data, boot recovery VMs, triage alerts. Designed for MSP technicians; no IDs to memorise.
long_description:
Slide MCP Server v5.0.0 connects Claude Desktop directly to your Slide BCDR / backup environment with a novice-first toolkit.

Claude reaches for this extension whenever you mention Slide, BCDR, business continuity, disaster recovery, backups, snapshots, restores, recovery VMs, file recovery, image exports, DR networks, audit logs, or unresolved alerts. You never have to spell out 'use the Slide MCP'.

What you can ask in plain English:
  * 'Are all my Slide boxes healthy?'
  * 'Did backups run last night for ACME?'
  * 'Find Q4-budget.xlsx on Bob's laptop and restore Tuesday's version.'
  * 'Boot a recovery VM for DC-01.'
  * 'What unresolved alerts do I have? Sort worst first.'
  * 'What changed in the last 24 hours?'

No IDs to memorise: every tool that needs an agent_id / device_id / client_id also accepts name_hint - pass a hostname, display name, or any substring ('Bob's laptop', 'ACME', 'DC-01') and the server fuzzy-resolves it server-side. Ambiguous matches return a structured list of candidates so the assistant can ask you to pick one.

New in v5.0.0:
  * slide_help tool (always available) for discovery, examples, glossary, and troubleshooting.
  * /slide.welcome slash-command prompt for first-time users.
  * slide://welcome / slide://help/glossary / slide://help/troubleshoot resources Claude can read at conversation start.
  * Server-side fuzzy name resolution via name_hint - no more 'a_xxxxxxxxxxxx' lookups.
  * Startup token validation with clear remediation steps for invalid tokens or firewall issues.
  * 'slide-mcp-server --doctor' subcommand for self-diagnosis (token, network, sample reads).
  * Every response now includes a next_steps array suggesting follow-up tool calls.
  * Friendlier error messages with did-you-mean suggestions for typo'd operations.

Keeps everything from v4: 11 task-oriented meta-tools (slide_overview / slide_files / slide_recovery / slide_audit / slide_clients / slide_admin / slide_agents / slide_devices / slide_snapshots / slide_backups / slide_alerts), 6 slash-command prompts, 11 slide:// resources, three permission tiers (read-only / safe / full).

This bundle includes signed and notarized binaries for macOS (Apple Silicon + Intel), Linux (amd64 + arm64), and Windows (amd64); the right binary is launched automatically for your machine.

Generate an API token at console.slide.tech under My Settings -> API Tokens. The token is stored by Claude Desktop and only sent to the slide-mcp-server process; it is never written into any project file.

api_key.title: Slide API Token
api_key.description: Generate at console.slide.tech under My Settings -> API Tokens. The token gives full access to your Slide account; it is stored by Claude Desktop and only forwarded to the slide-mcp-server process at launch.

tools_mode.title: Tool permissions
tools_mode.description: One of: read-only, safe, full. read-only = only list/get/search/browse. safe (default) = everything except deletes and device poweroff/reboot (restores, backup launch, agent/device settings all work). full = unrestricted, including deletes and device power-cycle. Legacy aliases reporting/restores/full-safe still accepted.
